originalTitle: Norway 1-2 England: World Cup quarter-final player ratings from Miami
summary: Jude Bellingham shines again for England but Orjan Håskjold Nyland, Norway’s goalkeeper hero against Brazil, makes the critical error
content: Norway Orjan Håskjold Nyland Norway’s hero against Brazil will have nightmares about his error that handed Bellingham his second goal. 3 Julian Ryerson Struggled to contain Gordon, but loves bombing forward. O’Reilly looked relieved when he was forced off. 8 Kristoffer Ajer The Brentford defender made some brilliant blocks and was almost the hero when he hit the bar. 7 Torbjørn Heggem Thought he had scored for a few minutes. England thought he got lucky for catching Bellingham. 6 David Møller Wolfe Dealt well with threat of Madueke and Saka for most of the game. Occasional forays forward before coming off. 6 Martin Ødegaard The heartbeat of Norway’s team grew in influence massively after a slow start. Gave everything for the cause. 8 Sander Berge Screened Norway’s back four tirelessly. No easy task in the sweltering conditions in Miami. 6 Patrick Berg Won ball off Kane for opening goal and a bustling presence throughout. Looks perfect for Premier League. 7 Alexander Sørloth Height always causes problems, although he dallied in the first half when a pass to Haaland was on. 6 Erling Haaland Opponents never feel safe when he’s around but looked totally devastated when he had to come off in extra time. 7 Andreas Schjelderup Only the Benfica winger will know if he actually meant his goal but they all count. 7 Substitutes: Fredrik Aursnes (for Ryerson, 60) Came to his side’s rescue with goalline clearance. 7; Oscar Bobb (for Sørloth, 68) Added dynamism down Norway’s right flank but could not make England pay in extra time. 6; Antonio Nusa (for Schjelderup, 68) Caused real problems when he came off the bench. 7; Marcus Pedersen (for Wolfe, 89) Struggled to contain Saka in extra time. 5; Leo Østigård (for Heggem, 90) Slow to react to the rebound when Bellingham scored England’s winner. 5; Jørgen Strand Larsen (for Haaland, 106) It’s not easy being Haaland’s understudy. Couldn’t make an impact. 5 England Jordan Pickford Looked shaky on occasions. Not clear why he doesn’t try to catch the ball more often. 6 Ezri Konsa Adapted well to new role at right-back, even if Schjelderup’s goal came down his side. 7 John Stones Got away with a real howler in the first half having not started since opening game. Yet recovered extremely well. 7 Marc Guéhi Immense battle with his Manchester City teammate Haalaad. Pulled off an epic block to stop a Nusa shot. 8 Nico O’Reilly Outstanding tackle to stop Bobb in full flow. Will have been disappointed to have been taken off. 7 Elliot Anderson Surging run set up first goal and dominated central midfield. Looks worth every penny Manchester City paid for him. 9 Declan Rice Clearly suffering from the effects of injury and illness. Not his usual bustling presence and set-piece delivery was poor. 5 Noni Madueke Failed to capitalise on early opportunities, even if he showed that he can use his right foot occasionally. 4 Jude Bellingham Is there anything he can’t do? Unbelievable composure for first goal and pure instinct for second. 9 Anthony Gordon A standout performance on the left flank and provided another excellent assist for Bellingham. 8 Harry Kane Hardly had a sight of goal but the captain still played his part all over the pitch. 7 Substitutes: Eberechi Eze (for Rice, ht) Inches away from the winner in normal time, could have done more 6; Bukayo Saka (for Madueke, ht) Created England’s best chance in second half and far more effective than his Arsenal teammate 8; Reece James (for Gordon, 71) Came on in midfield before moving to right-back. Needs more game time but can be a real asset 6; Djed Spence (for O’Reilly, 86) Made a really positive impact and felt hard done by to see penalty overturned 7; Morgan Rogers (for Konsa, 89) Added some real thrust and it was from his shot that Bellingham pounced in extra time 8; Dan Burn (for Bellingham, 111) His cameos off the bench are becoming the stuff of legend. Towering in the second half of extra time 7.
chineseTitle: 挪威1-2英格兰：迈阿密世界杯四分之一决赛球员评分
chineseSummary: 裘德·贝林厄姆再次为英格兰队闪耀，但挪威对阵巴西的门将英雄奥扬·哈斯科约德·尼兰却犯下了严重错误
chineseContent: 挪威 奥扬·哈斯克约德·尼兰 挪威队对阵巴西队的英雄将会为他的失误而做噩梦，贝林厄姆的第二个进球是由他的失误造成的。 3 朱利安·瑞尔森（Julian Ryerson） 努力遏制戈登，但喜欢向前轰炸。当奥莱利被迫离开时，他看上去松了口气。 8 克里斯托弗·阿耶尔 布伦特福德后卫做出了一些精彩的拦网，当他击中横梁时几乎成为英雄。 7 托比昂·赫格姆 (Torbjørn Heggem) 有几分钟以为自己已经进球了。英格兰队认为他很幸运能够抓住贝灵厄姆。 6 David Møller Wolfe 在比赛的大部分时间里很好地应对了Madueke 和Saka 的威胁。下车前偶尔会向前突进。 6 马丁·厄德高 挪威队的心跳在缓慢的开局后影响力大幅增长。为了事业付出了一切。 8 桑德·伯格不知疲倦地掩护挪威队的四后卫。在迈阿密闷热的环境下，这并不是一件容易的事。 6 帕特里克·伯格 (Patrick Berg) 从凯恩手中夺回球权，取得首个进球，并在整场比赛中表现出色。看起来非常适合英超联赛。 7 亚历山大·索洛斯 身高总是会带来问题，尽管他在上半场传球给哈兰德时表现不佳。 6 埃尔林·哈兰德 当他在场时，对手从来不会感到安全，但当他不得不在加时赛中下场时，对手们看起来完全崩溃了。 7 安德烈亚斯·施耶尔德鲁普 只有本菲卡边锋知道他的进球是否属实，但它们都很重要。 7 名替补球员：弗雷德里克·奥斯内斯（Fredrik Aursnes，瑞尔森队，60 岁）通过门线解围拯救了球队。 7；奥斯卡·博布（Sørloth，68岁）为挪威右翼增添了活力，但无法让英格兰在加时赛中付出代价。 6；安东尼奥·努萨（Antonio Nusa，代表施耶尔德鲁普，68岁）替补出场时造成了真正的问题。 7；马库斯·佩德森（沃尔夫，89岁）在加时赛中努力遏制萨卡。 5； Leo Østigård（代表赫格姆，90）当贝林汉姆为英格兰队进球时，他对篮板反应缓慢
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
